texto: He has seen and felt how solemn a thing it was to approach the gate of death, to enter the presence of God; and from that awful point of vision, he has contemplated the world, and life, and human responsibility, as they are; and he has come back like a spirit from another sphere, clothed with all the solemnities of eternity; to live now as one soon in reality to be there.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Octavius Winslow: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “He has seen and felt how solemn a thing it was to approach the gate of death, to enter the presen…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, christian, solemn pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao.
